meta_data.description:
The most powerful rich text editor for Bubble. Period.

Built on Tiptap.dev — the same engine behind Notion, GitLab, and Linear — this plugin gives you a professional-grade editor with 55+ actions, real-time collaboration, and full control over every detail.

✅ Demo: https://tiptap-demo.bubbleapps.io/version-test/doc/demo


𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟-𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘 𝗖𝗢𝗟𝗟𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡
Multiple users editing the same document, live — with cursors, names, and colors. Google Docs-style collaboration, right inside your Bubble app.
👉 Get set up in minutes at https://tiptap.rico.wtf


𝗧𝗘𝗫𝗧 𝗙𝗢𝗥𝗠𝗔𝗧𝗧𝗜𝗡𝗚
- Bold, Italic, Strikethrough, Underline
- Subscript & Superscript
- Highlight
- Text color, font family, font size
- Text alignment
- Headings H1 through H6

𝗥𝗜𝗖𝗛 𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗧𝗘𝗡𝗧
- Resizable images with drag & drop upload
- YouTube embeds
- Tables (insert, merge cells, headers — the works)
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting
- Blockquotes, horizontal rules
- Bullet lists, numbered lists, task lists
- Collapsible details/toggle sections
- Links and mentions

𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗨𝗦
Build your own menus — or use the built-in ones:
- Top menu — always visible
- Bubble menu — pops up on text selection
- Floating menu — appears on empty lines

𝗞𝗘𝗬𝗕𝗢𝗔𝗥𝗗 𝗦𝗛𝗢𝗥𝗧𝗖𝗨𝗧𝗦
Classical editor shortcuts AND markdown shortcuts. Type # for H1, > for blockquote, ``` for code blocks — or use ⌘+B, ⌘+I, and the rest.
https://tiptap.dev/api/keyboard-shortcuts

𝗢𝗨𝗧𝗣𝗨𝗧
- Clean HTML
- Plain text
- JSON

𝗕𝗨𝗜𝗟𝗧 𝗙𝗢𝗥 𝗕𝗨𝗕𝗕𝗟𝗘
- Autobinding support
- Undo / Redo
- Character count
- Placeholder text
- Drag handle
- Invisible characters toggle
- 55+ element actions to control every feature programmatically


🚀 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟-𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘 𝗗𝗢𝗖𝗨𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧 𝗦𝗘𝗥𝗩𝗘𝗥
Want Google Docs-style collaboration in your Bubble app? I run a hosted document server built specifically for this plugin. No infrastructure headaches — just plug in and go.

→ https://tiptap.rico.wtf


Questions? Feature requests? Drop them on the forum thread (link above).
meta_data.name: Tiptap.dev (Flourish)
meta_data.plugin_instructions:
1. Place the element on the page
2. Feed it some initial content (plain text or HTML)
3. Save the HTML as output

Extras:
- build and wire your own menu
- floating menu
- bubble menu
